danger_signals.visual_form: Moon icon in top-right corner displaying changing lunar phases
danger_signals.meaning: Tracks passage of time and signals shifts in enemy demon activity patterns and moods; full moon or dark moon likely indicates heightened supernatural danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Demon sprites appearing in dungeon and world map environments
danger_signals.meaning: Visual transition to battle arena signals immediate combat threat; enemy positioning and appearance communicate relative danger level

danger_signals.visual_form: Different demon sprite designs and visual characteristics
danger_signals.meaning: Visual design communicates demon type, alignment, and combat difficulty; unfamiliar appearances suggest unknown threat levels

danger_signals.visual_form: Story-triggered environmental anomalies and phenomena around the town
danger_signals.meaning: Visual environmental changes signal escalating supernatural threats that motivate party action

safety_signals.visual_form: Specific marked locations in town and story areas where player can save progress
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones where party can consolidate progress; visual distinction indicates sanctuaries from immediate threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Shifted perspective when navigating the students' hometown
safety_signals.meaning: Safe, familiar domestic space; perspective change signals transition from danger to relative security

safety_signals.visual_form: Special Velvet Room location where Igor provides Persona customization services
safety_signals.meaning: Neutral ground for power consolidation; visual access to Igor signals opportunity to strengthen party without combat pressure

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Persona summons representing individual internal selves
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hometown locations, school setting, familiar town spaces

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Individual demon designs and visual characteristics from Megami Tensei tradition
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple demon sprite designs with varying appearances communicating type and threat level

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears in narrative context to guide party; represents beneficial supernatural intervention

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Clear sky, normal lunar phases, familiar hometown appearances
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Demon encounter frequency and difficulty is lower; demon moods are less aggressive

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual environmental anomalies increase; lunar phase indicator shows transitional states
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Demon activity patterns shift based on moon phase; encounter frequency increases; demon aggression intensifies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Story-triggered visual manifestations of supernatural phenomena around town; environmental corruption indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Demon moods become more volatile; enemy encounter rates surge; enemies demonstrate more aggressive tactical positioning on battle grid

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Continuous moon phase progression in top-right icon (new moon → waxing → full → waning → new)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Demon activity patterns and moods are directly tied to current lunar phase; certain demons may only appear at specific moon phases

environmental_storytelling.technique: Lunar phase as narrative time marker
environmental_storytelling.example: The moon icon continuously displays passage of in-game time; players learn to associate specific moon phases with supernatural intensity without explicit exposition

Overhead view in hometown signals safety and familiarity; angled third-person in story locations creates slightly elevated tension; first-person dungeon navigation creates immersion and danger through perspective claustrophobia
Diverse demon sprite designs from Megami Tensei tradition communicate that supernatural threats are varied, complex, and embedded in mythological/psychological frameworks rather than generic evil
Enemy placement on grid-based arena communicates tactical intent and relative threat; surrounding player party visually signals ambush or coordinated attack

environmental_storytelling.technique: High school student setting as grounding device
environmental_storytelling.example: Familiar domestic/educational locations visually anchor supernatural events to real-world context, making horror/mystery more visceral

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Attacks the party; triggered by specific dialogue choices or actions; visual tell would be aggressive sprite animation
Flees the battle; triggered by intimidation or display of superior power; visual tell would be retreat animation
Gives the player an item or spell card (tarot card aligned with Persona family/Arcanum); triggered by flattery or appeasement; visual tell would be celebratory animation
Leaves battle, gives item, or grants special spell card; triggered by intrigue or curiosity; visual tell would be attentive/engaged animation
Party receives free turn of attacks costing no MP/HP; visual tell is positioning on grid before battle initiation
Character summons Persona to cast spell, heal, or deal damage; visual manifestation is summoning animation and spell effect

notes: Revelations: Persona uses a sophisticated multi-layered visual communication system grounded in lunar cycles and perspective shifts. The moon phase indicator is the primary world-state visual communicator, creating a dynamic danger environment where temporal progression visually tracks supernatural intensity. Combat grid positioning and demon behavioral animations provide tactical danger signaling. Perspective navigation (overhead/third-person/first-person) creates psychological distance modulation between safety and threat. The game communicates faction identity through narrative context and demon variety rather than explicit color/symbol systems. Demon emotional states are mechanically and visually tied to player dialogue choices, making enemy behavior communication central to gameplay. The Velvet Room provides a narrative-safe space visually separated from threat environments. Philemon represents benevolent supernatural guidance, narratively offsetting demon antagonism. The high school student protagonist framework grounds abstract supernatural threats in relatable domestic space, enhancing danger communication through contrast with familiar environments.
